stack.gpus: 2x AMD Radeon AI PRO R9700 (gfx1201, 32 GiB each)

method.metric: median across runs of average TPOT from API completion_tokens over the first-decode-event to finish interval; tok/s = 1000 / TPOT_ms
method.warning: A one-point 220K run decodes immediately after a full cold prefill. Compare scheduler arms only after priming the same prefix; incremental context sweeps and cold one-point runs are not interchangeable.
method.instrumentation_correction: The original harness counted nonempty SSE text events. Parser buffering made that invalid when FP8 activation quantization changed generated text/chunking. The harness was fixed and the auto-vs-Triton decision was rerun with API-reported completion-token counts.

laguna.kv_cache_dtype: checkpoint-selected FP8 E4M3 with static scales

auto_post087.dense_fp8_backend: auto (RDNA4 torch dequantize + BF16 GEMM fallback)
auto_post087.measurement: legacy nonempty-SSE-event rate; absolute values happened to agree within about 1% with the corrected auto rerun

dense_triton_legacy_sse_event_measuremen.invalid_reason: counted nonempty streamed text events rather than completion tokens; Triton changed output arithmetic and parser chunking
dense_triton_legacy_sse_event_measuremen.cache_state: increasing-depth sweep; final point was aborted based on what was later shown to be an instrumentation artifact

isolated_kernel_probe.conclusion: The kernel is viable; the corrected server A/B below confirms a large win even before exact-shape tuning.

points.comparison_caveat: Observed completion-token rate on different early-stopping continuations, not a fixed-output isolation

quality.comprehensive_text: 35/36; 8/8 code generation; only Python -7 % 3 convention missed
quality.multi_turn_tool_calls: 2/2 structured (read then todowrite), no marker leakage

dense_triton_token_counted.disposition: Promoted to the Laguna launch preset. Exact shared-expert shape tuning remains additional headroom, not a prerequisite.

attention_launch_ab.cache_state: one-point cold-prefix runs; compare directionally with the matching default run
attention_launch_ab.disposition: Keep BLOCK_N=32 and num_warps=4. BLOCK_H=8 is not a valid gfx1201 WMMA tile.

overlap_ab.disposition: No single-user deep-context win after cache-state control. Retain ENABLE_OVERLAP_SCHEDULE=1 as an opt-in for future concurrent/shared-prefix tests; keep preset default off.

profile_220277.warning: Profiler timing inflates short kernels; a separate get_num_kv_splits microbenchmark measured about 7 us/call, so its traced time is not actionable.

qwen3_coder_30b_fp8.measurement: legacy nonempty-SSE-event harness; use as a same-output baseline only until refreshed with completion-token accounting

dcp2_validity.local_tp2_models: invalid: all have num_kv_head_replicas=1 with dcp_size=2
dcp2_validity.reason: DCP all-gathers TP-sharded Q heads but leaves K/V local; adjacent DCP ranks must therefore hold identical replicated K/V heads.
hybrid_swa_additional_blockers: masked SWA KV writes are unsupported
dcp2_validity.disposition: Do not report DCP2 performance for the local TP2 coding models. Fail fast instead of producing fast but incorrect output.

verification.patch_replay: 62/62 patches applied strictly from f63458b5beaceabbd9d749b9fc956370e1b649e6; 7017 files/symlinks matched the live tree; no unexpected double-apply; patch 026 was the documented exception
verification.launcher_smoke: Laguna default selected fp8_gemm_runner_backend=triton, captured the batch-one graph, became healthy, and returned the requested FP8 READY response; GPUs idle after shutdown

ship: Laguna native Triton dense block-FP8 default with FP8_GEMM_BACKEND=auto rollback
next: tune the 39x-per-token shared-expert dense shapes with a gfx1201-safe correctness-gated normal-kernel tuner
profile the new native-FP8 default and resweep KV splits 48/64/80/96/128
benchmark cold and warm multi-turn/shared-prefix agentic traffic with p50/p95 TTFT and TPOT
expand query-selected sparse-decode quality testing to 30-50 coding/retrieval tasks
